task_completion.description: Open or edit the local_rules.xml file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a terminal or text editor (like nano or vim) with the file path /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml visible.

task_completion.description: Create a new rule with ID 100250 and level 0
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML text `<rule id="100250" level="0">` visible in the local_rules.xml file.

task_completion.description: Set the rule as a child of rule 5715
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML text `<if_sid>5715</if_sid>` visible within the rule 100250 block.

task_completion.description: Apply the rule to the user 'backup_svc'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML text `<user>backup_svc</user>` or `<match>backup_svc</match>` visible within the rule 100250 block.

task_completion.description: Set the rule to apply between 02:55 and 03:30
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML text `<time>02:55-03:30</time>` visible within the rule 100250 block.

task_completion.description: Restart the Wazuh manager to apply changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command such as `systemctl restart wazuh-manager` or `/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart` being executed, followed by a successful prompt return.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually modified the file on the system
integrity.visual_evidence: The file modification should happen in a real terminal text editor (nano, vim) or via valid shell commands (echo, sed), not just typed into a disconnected notepad.

integrity.description: Verify the restart command was actually executed
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot should show the terminal prompt returning after the restart command, indicating it was executed and not just typed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just echo a success message
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal history should show the actual configuration steps and restart command, rather than a script that simply prints 'Task completed'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml. The custom rule must have ID '100250' and level '0'. It must include a parent rule reference to '5715' (e.g., <if_sid>5715</if_sid>). The rule must target the user 'backup_svc' (e.g., <user>backup_svc</user> or <match>backup_svc</match>) and specify the time range '02:55-03:30' (e.g., <time>02:55-03:30</time>). Finally, the Wazuh manager must be restarted.
